ops.title: Harden ListPrefix/Resolve against go-git's silently-dropped-refs race
ops.description: Pre-existing flake exposed by CI on commit ee53865 (run 29919229985 predecessor; failing run 29919265860, passed on rerun): TestClaimRaceConcurrentActors had a racer exit 3 "entity not found" because go-git's dotgit walkReferencesTree (v5.19.1 dotgit.go:972-1009) silently skips a loose-ref file that vanishes mid-walk (git update-ref rename window) — returning a successful but incomplete list, which Resolve reports as ErrNotFound. Not introduced by the v0.39.0 storage-level open: Repository.References() was already exactly Storer.IterReferences(). retryEmptyRef only covers dotgit.ErrEmptyRefFile, not the silent-skip mode. Candidate fixes: retry zero-match full-id resolves once, or widen the retry helper around the walk. Relevant code: internal/gitobj/read.go (ListPrefix), internal/gitobj/gitobj.go (retryEmptyRef), internal/store/resolve.go.

ops.text: TestClaimRaceConcurrentActors passes repeatedly under forced loose-ref churn (e.g. stress run or injected rename window) without exit-3 not-found losers
ListPrefix or Resolve retries when a full-id lookup returns zero matches while the ref demonstrably exists (CAS updates racing)
